titleStmt.title: Transcription and Translation of NLS Adv. MS 15.1.18, no. 33

respStmt.resp: Exported from Models of Authority data
respStmt.name: tei_exporter

availability.p: Status unknown. Exported for research and migration workflows.

revisionDesc.change: transcription moa=116 status=live modified=2017-01-30 19:28:00.473884+00 | translation moa=117 status=live modified=2016-06-07 13:00:13.582348+00

div.p:
. P . ⁊ W. abbates de Dunfermelin ⁊ de lundores . ⁊ h . prior de pethneweme  Omnibus ad quorum noticiam ⁊ intellectum tenor presentis scripti peruenerit  salutem in domino .
Nouerit fraternitas uestra [nos] mandato domini pape Innocentii suscepto nobis iniunctum fuisse  ut questionem quam Ranulfus archidiaconus de sancto andrea proponebat aduersus hugonem
seneschaldum ⁊ Jordanum nepotem ipsius super terris ex occidentalis parte noui operis existentibus uersus domos lamburc quas asserebat ad archidiaconatum suum specta
re  conuocatis partibus ⁊ auditis vel iudicio decideremus  vel compositione interueniente liti finem imponeremus . Conuocatis igitur aliquociens partibus tandem mediante
domino episcopo sancti andree Willelmo . ⁊ partes suas interponente nobis etiam auctoritatem prestantibus ⁊ operam ⁊ diligentiam adhibentibus  compositum est inter partes ita quod
hugo seneschaldus ⁊ Jordanus nepos eius resignauerunt ⁊ quietas in posterum clamauerunt terras illas cum edificiis in eis ab ipsis constructis . R . archidiacono ⁊ succes
soribus eius . Ipse uero . R . archidiaconus pro resignatione facienda ⁊ pro bono pacis dedit hugoni seneschaldo . xv . marcas . ⁊ Jordano . x . marcas ⁊ quatuor perticatas
terre quas a . T . priore ⁊ eius conuentu acceperat proximas domui symonis clerici ex parte orientali tenendas de se ⁊ successoribus suis sibi ⁊ heredibus suis soluendo
annuatim unam libram cimini . ⁊ ut hec compositio rata consistat ⁊ imperpetuum inconcussa permaneat inuiolabiliter obseruanda  tam sigilli domini episcopi quam nostrorum
appositione est corroborata ⁊ confirmata . His testibus . Domino . T . priore ⁊ eius conuentu . Andrea de morauia . Magistro Adam . Magistro Stephano . Magistro ysaac .
Magistro Symone . Willelmo de karel decano . Willelmo capellano de Brechhin . Willelmo de deruesin . Petro ⁊ Eadwardo capellanis domini episcopi . Johanne de hautuisel .
Willelmo de Colin . Johanne de londiniis . Radulfo nigro . Johanne maraschaldo . Symone de nuisi . Eutropio dispensatore . Petro ⁊ hugone de nidin ⁊ Roberto
de kalledouer pueris domini episcopi . Willelmo lambin . Gileberto Britone . Symone clerico .

p.cl: Patrick and Wido/Guy, abbots of Dunfermline and of Lindores, and Hugh, prior of Pittenweem : To all to whose attention and understanding the content of the present written document shall have come : Greeting in the Lord.

that, with the mandate of Innocent the lord Pope having been received by us, we have been enjoined either to decide by a judgment the dispute which Ranulf, archdeacon of St Andrews, brought against Hugh the Steward and Jordan his nephew over the lands on the west side of the new work standing opposite the dwellings of Lamburc, and which he was claiming to belong to his own archdeaconry, having called together and heard the parties; or to impose an end to the dispute by mediating an agreement. The parties therefore having been called together several times, with William, lord bishop of St Andrews, acting as mediator, and standing between the two sides, with us also discharging authority and employing effort and diligence, it was agreed between the parties so that Hugh the Steward and Jordan his nephew resigned and quitclaimed those lands for the future, and the buildings in them that were built by them, to Ranulf the archdeacon and to his successors. And Ranulf the archdeacon himself has given to Hugh the Steward, in return for making the resignation and for the sake of peace, 15 merks, and to Jordan 10 merks and four perches of land that he had received from Thomas the prior and his community near the dwelling of Simon the clerk on the east side, for Jordan and his heirs to hold of him and his successors by paying one pound of cumin yearly.

And so that this authoritative agreement should stand firm and remain unchanged in perpetuity, to be observed without violation, it has been reinforced and made firm by the application of the seal of the lord bishop as well as our own.

With these witnesses: the lord Thomas the prior, and the community; Andrew of Moray; Master Adam; Master Stephen; Master Isaac; Master Simon; William of Crail, dean; William, chaplain of Brechin; William of Dairsie; Peter and Edward, the chaplains of the lord bishop; John of Haltwhistle; William of Gullane; John of London; Ralph Niger; John the marischal; Simon de Noisy; Eutrope the dispenser; Peter and Hugh of Nydie and Robert of Calder, man-servants of the lord bishop; William Lambin; Gilbert Breton; Simon the clerk.
